title: Rimaria microsperma
taxonomy: Rimaria microsperma is classified as a species of lichen within the family Lecanoraceae. This lichen genus is primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions, with its exact classification belonging to the phylum Ascomycota. Further classification includes its membership in the class Lecanoromycetes, order Lecanorales, and family Lecanoraceae. The genus Rimaria is known to comprise several species of lichens, with R. microsperma being one of them. Its classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Rimaria microsperma is a species of lichen that has been found to have potential medicinal properties. Its extracts have been studied for their antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which could be beneficial in treating various infections.
